title: Hardware-Based Adaptive Learning
abstract: We present Hardware-Based Adaptive Learning, a neuromorphic approach to on-chip learning acceleration for mask-locked inference systems. While traditional mask-locked cartridges have immutable weights (baked into metal at fabrication), we introduce plastic regions—reconfigurable memory arrays that enable local learning without full network retraining. By implementing spike-timing-dependent plasticity (STDP), Hebbian learning, and reward-modulated plasticity in mixed
comments: 25 pages, 10 figures, submitted to arXiv on 2026-03-15
